action.hypothesis: Run the newly added gated SAE prototype at the existing K=128 anchor to get the first quality signal on whether separate support gating can materially improve FVU over plain TopK.
action.summary: Execute the first proxy run for the gated architecture family at K=128 to establish whether this new family deserves incubation.
action.self_review: No new code changes this round. This is intentionally a clean first measurement of the gated family before adding family-specific sweeps or cost work.
notes_to_memory: Round 2 requests the first actual proxy measurement for the gated SAE prototype at the K=128 anchor.
No recent results were available, so the correct next step is to execute the new family rather than branch into further tuning.
Keep evaluation focused on FVU improvement versus the current TopK anchor; do not reward smaller K or efficiency yet.
next_hypotheses: If gated improves proxy FVU, do a gated-family stabilization pass on gate sharpness or magnitude defaults.
If gated is quality-neutral but slower, separate implementation cost from architectural value before discarding it.
If gated clearly regresses FVU, pivot to a different architecture family rather than spending many rounds on local tuning.
